page: Microsoft Azure F64-16amds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Famdsv7 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, running under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The server is configured with 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. It also includes 7559 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that require substantial local disk capacity.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, scoring in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core tests, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average range. Memory performance is strong, with the PassMark memory score placing in the top 25%, though uncached large-block memory bandwidth read and write speeds remain average. For specialized workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds for small language models and strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F64-16amds_v7 offers high resource density by pairing a moderate vCPU count with an exceptionally large memory footprint and massive local storage. This profile minimizes vCPU licensing costs while providing the memory capacity required for large datasets. The server is well-suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale data caching, enterprise applications, and local data processing tasks that do not require GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure F64-16amds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Famdsv7 family, featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing an exceptional 32.0 GB of memory per core, alongside 7559 GB of local storage. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this server delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core and single-threaded compression benchmarks. While multi-core performance and memory bandwidth align with average tiers, its PassMark memory score is strong. The high memory-to-CPU ratio and massive local storage capacity make this server highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads without the need for high vCPU counts. It is best suited for large databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F64-16amds_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 7559 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for database and caching workloads.

tagline: A memory-intensive virtual server combining dedicated AMD EPYC processing with massive RAM capacity and extensive local storage.
